title: Why 'One Battle After Another' Won at the Oscars
text: Paul Thomas Anderson's "One Battle After Another" emerged victorious at the 98th Academy Awards, securing six Oscars including Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Adapted Screenplay. This marks Anderson's first time achieving this "trifecta" after previous near-misses with films like "There Will Be Blood" and "Phantom Thread". The film built overwhelming support across multiple voter groups. According to the analysis, it "won when it counted" after its Gotham Award victory, demonstrating widespread appeal. The film succeeded because it "assembled them together in a way that satisfied a range of audiences, from critics to costume designers." In the Academy's voting system, "One Battle After Another" benefited from strong placement across ballots. The article notes that even viewers who didn't rank it as their favorite typically placed it "in most peoples' Top 3 or even Top 5." Unlike competitor "Sinners"—a vampire film—"One Battle After Another" faced fewer obstacles related to genre bias.
